evidence:
Kimi claims firstLlmDeltaReceived is never removed and only cleared on close(), so a second turn with the same contextId skips latencyFiller.spliceLlmDelta. This misreads the code. grep of all references in packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.ts shows THREE removal sites, two of them per-turn:
  :390  this.firstLlmDeltaReceived.clear();   // dispose()/finalize teardown, NOT close-only-for-correctness
  :808  this.firstLlmDeltaReceived.delete(contextId);  // inside flushTtsText()
  :927  this.firstLlmDeltaReceived.delete(pkt.contextId); // inside handleInterruptDetected()
The per-turn clear is at :808. flushTtsText is called from handleLlmDone (:763 `const spokenText = this.flushTtsText(pkt.contextId);`), and handleLlmDone is bound to the bus llm.done event (:479 `this.bus.on("llm.done", this.handleLlmDone.bind(this));`). So on normal turn completion (llm.done) the contextId entry is deleted at :808 -- exactly the clear kimi recommends adding. The barge-in path also deletes it: interrupt.detected -> handleInterruptDetected (:489/:924) -> :927. The only branch where flushTtsText is skipped is handleLlmDone's early return when interruptedGenerationContextIds.has(contextId) is true (:757-761); but that flag is only ever set by handleInterruptDetected (:925), which already deleted the entry at :927 in the same handler -- so no orphan. The add side at :733-739 re-adds fresh on each turn's first delta and gates spliceLlmDelta; with the per-turn delete in place, the second turn re-enters the `!has` branch and splices normally. Kimi's premise (never removed) is factually false.
real_fix: No fix needed. The suggested fix (delete on llm.done) is already implemented: handleLlmDone -> flushTtsText -> firstLlmDeltaReceived.delete(contextId) at packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.ts:808, plus the barge-in delete at :927 and the dispose-time clear at :390. The per-turn first-delta filler splice is correctly re-armed each turn even when a client reuses a contextId.
